skill.name: agent-browser
skill.description: A headless browser automation skill for using agent-browser to navigate pages, interact with elements, capture snapshots, and manage sessions.
skill.summary: Control web pages through structured agent-browser commands for testing, extraction, and form workflows.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: AI review confirms high-risk guidance for using a real Chrome profile and persistent OAuth sessions through remote debugging. Most external-command hits are documentation examples for the intended CLI, while the weak-crypto and system-reconnaissance hits are false positives. No prompt injection or confirmed exfiltration endpoint was found, so this is high risk but not a confirmed malicious block.

high_findings.title: Logged-In Browser Profile Exposure
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs use of a real Chrome profile with remote debugging, which can expose live OAuth sessions and account access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly reference a real Chrome profile path and state that existing Google and Discord sessions are reused. This is direct evidence of sensitive browser profile access.

high_findings.title: Cookie and Local Storage Access in Logged-In Sessions
high_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The advanced commands include cookie and local storage access, which can reveal session tokens when paired with persisted logins.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command list directly includes cookies and local storage access. The earlier session guidance makes the sensitive context clear.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Is Core Behavior
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with legitimate purpose: The skill tells agents to install and run an external browser CLI, so users must trust the package and commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plain documentation for the stated browser automation tool, not hidden execution. Risk remains because agents may run package installation and browser commands.

medium_findings.title: Page JavaScript Evaluation Capability
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The eval command can run JavaScript in a page context and may modify or read data from authenticated pages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented eval command is explicit. It is a legitimate automation feature, but it becomes risky with authenticated browser state.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Demonstration URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for malicious networking: The URLs are examples for installation and browser navigation, not evidence of exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in visible examples and match the browser automation purpose. No evidence shows secrets being sent to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: No weak cryptographic algorithm is shown at the flagged lines; the matches are prose or option text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain the description and a JSON output option. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hit Is False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The flagged line documents finding elements by test id, not collecting host or system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is a browser selector helper for test IDs. No evidence found of uname, whoami, network interface, or host reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Debugging Against Real Chrome Profile
dangerous_patterns.description: The guidance combines Chrome remote debugging with a real user profile, which can give an agent access to authenticated browser state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented command uses remote debugging and a user data directory. The surrounding text confirms reuse of logged-in sessions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Browser Data Controls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents eval, cookies, and local storage commands that can inspect or change data in a live browser page.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These controls are explicitly listed. Their risk is heightened by the separate guidance for persistent OAuth sessions.

content.user_title: Automate Browser Workflows with Agent Browser
content.value_statement: Manual browser testing and data extraction can slow agent workflows. This skill provides structured commands for navigation, interaction, snapshots, and session control.
actual_capabilities: Install and run the agent-browser CLI through documented shell commands.
Open pages, navigate history, reload pages, and manage browser tabs.
Click, fill, type, hover, select, check, upload, and drag page elements.
Capture snapshots, screenshots, PDFs, text, HTML, values, and attributes.
Use persistent sessions and Chrome DevTools Protocol connections for browser state.
Return machine-readable snapshots for agent workflows with JSON output.
limitations: Requires agent-browser and its browser dependencies to be installed locally.
Authenticated sessions can expose sensitive cookies, storage, and account access.
Selectors and snapshot refs can change after navigation or page reloads.
The skill documents commands but does not provide its own sandbox or permission controls.

use_cases.title: Validate Web UI Flows
use_cases.description: Run browser actions, inspect page state, and capture evidence during feature testing.

use_cases.title: Collect Page Data
use_cases.description: Navigate target pages and extract visible text, attributes, screenshots, or PDFs.

use_cases.title: Automate Repeated Forms
use_cases.description: Fill fields, press keys, upload files, and verify completion across browser sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Open and Snapshot a Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to open the target URL, take an interactive snapshot, and summarize the visible page sections.

prompt_templates.title: Test a Form Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to fill the form fields, submit the form, and verify the resulting confirmation state.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Structured Page Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Use snapshots and get commands to collect product names, prices, links, and availability from the page.

prompt_templates.title: Debug an Authenticated Workflow Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use an isolated browser session to test the logged-in flow without accessing personal Chrome profiles or exporting cookies.

output_examples.input: Open a product page and inspect the visible content.
output_examples.output: The browser opened the page, captured a snapshot, and reported the title, main content areas, and visible action buttons.

output_examples.input: Fill a contact form and confirm the result.
output_examples.output: The form fields were completed, the submit action was triggered, and the page displayed a confirmation message.

output_examples.input: Capture evidence from a dashboard page.
output_examples.output: A screenshot was saved and the current URL, page title, and selected text values were summarized.

best_practices: Use isolated browser sessions for automation instead of personal browser profiles.
Take a fresh snapshot after each navigation before selecting elements by ref.
Limit automation to trusted sites and avoid exposing cookies or local storage.
anti_patterns: Do not connect the agent to a real personal Chrome profile for routine work.
Do not run eval commands on authenticated pages unless the JavaScript is fully reviewed.
Do not treat snapshot refs as stable after page reloads or navigation.

faq.question: What does this skill help an agent do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent control web pages with agent-browser commands for navigation, interaction, extraction, and screenshots.

faq.question: Does it require local software?
faq.answer: Yes. The agent-browser CLI and browser dependencies must be installed before the commands can run.

faq.question: Can it use logged-in sessions?
faq.answer: The skill documents persistent sessions and Chrome profile access, but personal profiles create serious privacy and account risks.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive accounts?
faq.answer: It should only be used with isolated test accounts or sandbox sessions. Avoid personal accounts and production credentials.

faq.question: Can it extract page data?
faq.answer: Yes. It can get text, HTML, values, attributes, counts, screenshots, PDFs, cookies, and local storage through documented commands.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk high?
faq.answer: The skill includes guidance for remote debugging with a real Chrome profile and accessing browser session data.
